ajoute.description: [Traduction en cours] Adds a value to a ~l:variable|k|core!variable~ or adds two ~m:values~ and puts the result into a ~m:variable~. If you add to a ~m: variable~ it must already hold a numeric value, but if you assign a ~m:variable~ to hold the result of an addition it will lose whatever value it previously held.%0a%0aAll floating-point values are held as strings.
ajoute.examples: ajoute float `1.561` à/a N%0aajoute N à/a Total donnant Total

soustrais/retire.description: [Traduction en cours] Subtracts a value from a variable, or subtracts one value from another and puts the result into a variable.
soustrais/retire.examples: soustrais/retire 5 depuis Count%0asoustrais/retire X depuis Y donnant Z

multiplie.description: [Traduction en cours] Multiplies a variable by a value, or multiplies two values and puts the result into a variable.
multiplie.examples: multiplie X par Y%0amultiplie X par 2 donnant Y

divise.description: [Traduction en cours] Divides a ~mono:variable~ by a ~mono:value~, or divides one ~mono:value~ by another, putting the result into a ~mono:variable~.
divise.examples: divise X par 2%0adivise X par Y donnant Z

FLOAT.description: [Traduction en cours] The ~m:float~ package contains a set of floating-point commands; initially just ~l:add~, ~l:subtract~, ~l:multiply~ and ~l:divide~.%0a%0aFloating-point values are held as strings such as `3.14159365`. The commands are identical to their integer versions except for the word ~m:float~ after the command name.
